title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, Text Constructs, and Extensions · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds format-aware syndication model from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of format-aware syndication model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Model RSS channel and item requirements, GUID semantics, dates, links, authors, categories, enclosures, source, and optional variance.
Model Atom feed and entry IDs, authors, links, text and content constructs, categories, dates, xml:base, xml:lang, and source.
Interpret expanded namespace names independently from chosen prefixes and preserve unsupported extension provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Rss Semantics
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 4882 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove RSS and Atom semantics, namespaces, content variance, and extension restraint; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Rss Semantics.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model rss channel and item requirements, guid semantics, dates, links, authors, categories, enclosures, source, and optional variance. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Rss Semantics, keep Feed Ts Rss Semantics connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
